story_title: Strait of Hormuz / Project Freedom paused
story_one_liner: Same Hormuz deal, five completely different stories — could every one be telling the truth?
music_brief: ambient drone bed throughout; intro sting handles open

world_tickers.headline: Japan to buy 20m UAE oil bypassing Hormuz
US trade gap widens as oil exports surge
Lufthansa fuel bill +€1.7B on Iran war
Pyongyang gas now pricier than Seoul

scenes.voiceover: Five countries. One Iran deal. Same facts, five very different stories.
The U.S. and Iran agreed a one-page memo to end the Hormuz war. Pakistan brokered. Oil fell eleven percent.
Reuters kept it neutral. Trump pauses operation. Iran says safe passage possible.
scenes.headline_quoted: U.S. and Iran closing in on memorandum to end war

scenes.voiceover: America heard victory and threat in one sentence.
scenes.headline_quoted: If they don't agree, the bombing starts at a much higher level than before.
Beijing will play a greater role in ending Mideast fighting.
The United States has suffered a shameful defeat.

scenes.voiceover: And the paradox. Foreign Affairs — the establishment magazine of U.S. foreign policy — agreed with Russian state media. Both said: Iran proved it can close Hormuz. The IRGC stays in place.
scenes.headline_quoted: Iran proved it can close the strait, even in the face of significant military force.

scenes.voiceover: Same facts. Five stories. Could every one of them be telling the truth?
scenes.on_screen_text:
Same facts. Five stories.
Daily Framings — follow.

fact_check_provenance.primary_event_source: Pakistani diplomatic source via Reuters, confirmed by Axios

supporting_quotes_per_frame.exact_headline: U.S. and Iran closing in on memorandum to end war, sources say
If they don't agree, the bombing starts at a much higher level than before
Beijing to play 'greater role' in ending Mideast fighting
The United States has suffered a shameful defeat
Iran proved it can close the strait, even in the face of significant military force — Foreign Affairs (agreeing with RT)
